content.assessment: Applying a whole-system reform lens, T1 represents the absence of any policy bundle: it relies entirely on informal, non-structural adjustment mechanisms (ad hoc negotiation, uneven local social capital) rather than coordinated statutory reform. The lens's core insight from the Portuguese case — that observed gains cannot be attributed to any single isolated intervention — implies the reverse is also true: absence of a coordinated bundle predicts absence of systemic improvement, and existing evidence bears this out. The reliance on informal mobilization (F-live-portuguese_reform-10) produces quality and equity levelling failures by construction, since bonding/bridging network variation across villages with similar demographics generates arbitrary rather than criteria-based outcomes. Meanwhile the unchecked growth of selective church-run schooling (F-live-portuguese_reform-08) alongside catchment-bound state schools structurally worsens quality levelling and equity, and does so against a live legal constraint (F-live-portuguese_reform-09) that a status-quo trajectory does not address.
opportunities: Could still serve as a baseline against which the marginal value of coordinated reform bundles (T2-T5) is measured
strengths: No new administrative burden or transition risk since existing informal mechanisms continue unchanged
threats: Persistent reliance on non-structural, ad hoc equity outcomes risks entrenching the very segregation dynamics now under legal challenge (F-live-portuguese_reform-09, F-live-portuguese_reform-08)
Absence of any bundled or coordinated reform makes causal improvement in quality or equity unlikely, consistent with the lens's premise that isolated/no intervention cannot replicate systemic gains
weaknesses: Non-structural, informal survival mechanisms (local social capital) demonstrably produce uneven outcomes across similarly-situated villages rather than equitable, rule-based levelling (F-live-portuguese_reform-10)

Continued unchecked growth of selective church-run schools alongside catchment-bound state schools concentrates disadvantage without any counterbalancing coordinated policy (F-live-portuguese_reform-08)
No coordinated reform bundle exists to address the underlying legal exposure on segregation established by ECtHR and EU infringement proceedings (F-live-portuguese_reform-09)
